req.title: System-Admin views payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a payroll-run through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-040 requires that an authenticated system-admin can view a payroll-run via the UI at /payroll-runs, that mandatory fields are validated, an audit record is written on success, and that proper HTTP error codes (400, 401, 403) are returned on error paths. I derived: (1) a positive EP test covering the happy path for an authenticated admin viewing an existing payroll-run; (2) a NEG test for an unauthenticated request expecting 401; (3) a NEG/DT test for a forbidden role (non-admin) expecting 403; (4) a BVA test targeting the boundary of a payroll-run with the minimum valid field set (exactly the mandatory fields populated, none extra); (5) a STC test verifying the state transition from 'draft' payroll-run to 'viewed' with an audit record written. Each test is self-contained via fixtures and helper placeholders.

tests.name: test_admin_views_existing_payroll_run_returns_200_and_audit
preconditions: At least one payroll-run record exists in the system
actions: Obtain a valid admin session token via <fixture: admin_auth_token>
Retrieve a known payroll-run id via <helper_fn: get_existing_payroll_run_id>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} with Authorization header set to admin token
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log via <helper_fn: get_latest_audit_record> for the payroll-run id
expected: Response body contains the payroll-run entity id
Audit record exists with action='VIEW' and entity_id matching the payroll-run id
trace: R-WEB-040: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_views_existing_payroll_run_returns_200_and_audit(
    admin_auth_token,
    api_client,
    get_existing_payroll_run_id,
    get_latest_audit_record,
):
    payroll_run_id = get_existing_payroll_run_id()
    response = api_client.get(
        f"/payroll-runs/{payroll_run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("id") == payroll_run_id
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_id=payroll_run_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["entity_id"] == payroll_run_id
    assert audit["action"] == "VIEW"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_to_view_payroll_run_returns_401
preconditions: At least one payroll-run record exists in the system
actions: Retrieve a known payroll-run id via <helper_fn: get_existing_payroll_run_id>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} with NO Authorization header
Capture HTTP response status
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_request_to_view_payroll_run_returns_401(
    api_client,
    get_existing_payroll_run_id,
):
    payroll_run_id = get_existing_payroll_run_id()
    response = api_client.get(
        f"/payroll-runs/{payroll_run_id}",
    )
    assert response.status_code == 401
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_view_payroll_run_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not admin)
At least one payroll-run record exists in the system
actions: Obtain a valid non-admin session token via <fixture: employee_auth_token>
Retrieve a known payroll-run id via <helper_fn: get_existing_payroll_run_id>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} with Authorization header set to employee token
Capture HTTP response status
expected: Response body contains a forbidden/authorization error message
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_admin_role_view_payroll_run_returns_403(
    employee_auth_token,
    api_client,
    get_existing_payroll_run_id,
):
    payroll_run_id = get_existing_payroll_run_id()
    response = api_client.get(
        f"/payroll-runs/{payroll_run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_auth_token}"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 403
    body = response.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_admin_views_payroll_run_with_minimum_mandatory_fields_returns_200
preconditions: A payroll-run record exists containing exactly the minimum set of mandatory fields
actions: Obtain a valid admin session token via <fixture: admin_auth_token>
Create (or retrieve) a payroll-run with only mandatory fields via <helper_fn: create_minimal_payroll_run>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} with Authorization header set to admin token
Capture HTTP response status and body
Verify all mandatory fields are present in the response
expected: Response body contains the payroll-run entity id
All mandatory fields are present and non-null in the response body
No optional fields cause a validation error
trace: R-WEB-040: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest

MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["id", "run_date", "status", "period_start", "period_end"]

def test_admin_views_payroll_run_with_minimum_mandatory_fields_returns_200(
    admin_auth_token,
    api_client,
    create_minimal_payroll_run,
):
    payroll_run_id = create_minimal_payroll_run()
    response = api_client.get(
        f"/payroll-runs/{payroll_run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("id") == payroll_run_id
    for field in MANDATORY_FIELDS:
        assert field in body, f"Mandatory field '{field}' missing from response"
        assert body[field] is not None, f"Mandatory field '{field}' is null"

tests.name: test_state_transition_draft_to_viewed_writes_audit_record
preconditions: A payroll-run record exists in 'draft' state and has not yet been viewed
actions: Obtain a valid admin session token via <fixture: admin_auth_token>
Create a payroll-run in 'draft' state via <helper_fn: create_draft_payroll_run>
Confirm initial state is 'draft' via <helper_fn: get_payroll_run_state>
Send GET /payroll-runs/{id} with Authorization header set to admin token
Capture HTTP response status and body
Re-query payroll-run state via <helper_fn: get_payroll_run_state>
Query audit log via <helper_fn: get_latest_audit_record> for VIEW action
expected: Payroll-run state transitions to 'viewed' after the GET request
Audit record exists recording the VIEW transition with correct entity_id and actor
trace: R-WEB-040: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_state_transition_draft_to_viewed_writes_audit_record(
    admin_auth_token,
    api_client,
    create_draft_payroll_run,
    get_payroll_run_state,
    get_latest_audit_record,
    admin_user_id,
):
    payroll_run_id = create_draft_payroll_run()
    initial_state = get_payroll_run_state(payroll_run_id)
    assert initial_state == "draft"
    response = api_client.get(
        f"/payroll-runs/{payroll_run_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    post_state = get_payroll_run_state(payroll_run_id)
    assert post_state == "viewed"
    audit = get_latest_audit_record(entity_id=payroll_run_id, action="VIEW")
    assert audit is not None
    assert audit["entity_id"] == payroll_run_id
    assert audit["actor_id"] == admin_user_id
